title: 10 precautions to take when using Sambucus nigra (Elder) for medicinal purposes
intro: When using Sambucus nigra, also known as elderberry, for medicinal purposes, it is important to take certain precautions. First, only use carefully prepared elderberry products, as the raw berries, bark, and leaves of the plant contain a substance that produces cyanide and can be toxic. It is recommended to use elderberry products that have been properly processed and standardized. Pregnant and breastfeeding women should consult with a healthcare provider before using elderberry. Additionally, people with autoimmune diseases should use caution when taking elderberry, as it may stimulate the immune system and potentially worsen symptoms. Lastly, if you have any concerns or experience adverse effects while taking elderberry, it is important to seek medical attention immediately.
